---
title: "AI Agents in Economics and Business Research"
subtitle: "Day 1 - Foundations"
author: "Tinbergen Institute"
format:
  revealjs:
    footer: "Tinbergen Institute | Day 1"
---

## {.center}

::: {.r-fit-text}
Build strong foundations first.
:::

## Day 1 goals

- Understand LLM mechanics
- Define practical AI agents
- Prompt with clear structure

## LLMs: what matters

- Pattern completion at scale
- Context shapes every answer
- Sampling controls output behavior

## AI agents: minimal definition

- LLM plus task context
- LLM plus tool access
- LLM plus memory loops

## Context engineering essentials

- Define task and constraints
- Provide examples and format
- Keep context clean, specific

## MCP and tools

- Models can call APIs
- Tools expand real capabilities
- Reliability needs guardrails

## IDE agents in practice

- Scope tasks into chunks
- Verify generated code quickly
- Keep git history clean

## Danger zones

- Never leak private data
- Watch copyright and licensing
- Avoid automation overtrust

## Task {.center background-color="black"}

**Exercise 1: Prompt Repair**

- Open `Live Exercises`
- Submit one improved prompt
- Include one failure mode

*Groups of 3. 12 minutes.*

## Task {.center background-color="black"}

**Exercise 2: Coding Agent Sprint**

- Use VS Code or Cursor
- Complete one micro task
- Submit approach and output

*Groups of 3. 15 minutes.*

## Wrap-up

- Foundation beats tool hype
- Clear prompts reduce errors
- Verification is non-negotiable
